name: HandleCommand
documentation:
Marks a method or constructor as a handler for command messages ({@link MessageType#COMMAND}).

Command handlers typically represent intent to perform an action or change state. They often return a result, which is published to the result log unless marked {@code passive}.

This annotation is a concrete specialization of {@link HandleMessage} for commands.

@see HandleMessage @see MessageType#COMMAND

methods.name: allowedClasses
methods.documentation: Restricts which payload types this handler may be invoked for.

methods.name: disabled
methods.documentation: If {@code true}, disables this handler during discovery.

methods.name: onMissingProtectedData
methods.documentation: Determines how the handler responds when referenced protected data is no longer available.

methods.name: passive
methods.documentation:
If {@code true}, this handler is considered passive and will not emit a result message.
 Useful when the handler is for side effects only.

methods.name: skipExpiredRequests
methods.documentation:
If {@code true}, indexed request messages whose effective timeout has already expired may be skipped before this handler is invoked.

Defaults to {@code false} so a sender-side timeout does not change command execution semantics. Enable this only for commands that are safe to drop when they are stale.
